content: 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as a distributed collective of semi-autonomous micro-agents, where each micro-slot in the agent's memory architecture acts as an independent curious agent that selectively votes on which new unlabeled data to acquire for the whole system. This distributed memory governance design leverages the intrinsic curiosity of individual micro-agents to outperform single- controller active selection at identifying long-tail useful information.
